title: 10 Herbal Creams For Itchy Eyes
intro_desc: If you're suffering from itchy eyes, you might want to give herbal creams a try. These creams are made from plants that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help calm down the itchiness. For example, Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, is often used in eye creams because it contains compounds that can reduce swelling and ease irritation. Calendula officinalis, or marigold, is another herb that's commonly used in eye care products due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It can help soothe the eyes and reduce redness. Echinacea purpurea, a popular herb used to boost the immune system, also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate itchy eyes.  These herbal creams work by providing a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, which can help prevent irritants from getting in and causing more itchiness. They can also help reduce puffiness and redness, making your eyes look and feel better. When you use a herbal cream for itchy eyes, you can experience relief from the discomfort and frustration that comes with it. You'll be able to go about your day without feeling like you need to rub your eyes constantly, and you can even get some restful sleep without waking up to itchiness. By giving your eyes some relief from itchiness, these herbal creams can improve your quality of life and make you feel more comfortable and relaxed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains allantoin, aloe-emodin, and symphytine as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm itchy eyes. Allantoin, in particular, has been shown to reduce swelling and promote healing, making it an effective ingredient for soothing irritated eyes. Aloe-emodin has antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing inflammation and discomfort. When applied as a cream, these active constituents work together to provide relief from itchy, irritated eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of Symphytum officinale extract, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add the aloe vera gel and Symphytum officinale extract to the saucepan. Stir until well combined.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and slowly add the distilled water while stirring continuously.
Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, it is ready to use as an eye cream for itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains flavonoids, triterpenoids, and carotenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The flavonoids, specifically quercetin, have a natural antihistamine effect that reduces itching and redness. The triterpenoids, such as saponins, have antimicrobial properties that prevent infections and promote healing of the eye surface. By reducing inflammation and preventing infections, Calendula officinalis creams can help to alleviate itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the coconut oil in the saucepan over low heat and add the Calendula flowers.
Let the mixture simmer for 2-3 hours, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a saucepan over low heat until melted.
Combine the Calendula oil with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture, and pour into a container to cool and set.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains alkylamides and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and redness in itchy eyes. The alkylamides also have an inhibitory effect on histamine release, a chemical that can exacerbate itchy eyes. Echinacea's flavonoids, particularly cichoric acid, have antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm itchy eyes. By reducing inflammation and histamine release, Echinacea purpurea creams can provide relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, stirring until smooth.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 2 hours, or until it reaches room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Add the vitamin E oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains hyperforin and flavonoids, which are active constituents that help reduce inflammation and itching in itchy eyes. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the eye area, providing relief from irritation and discomfort. Hyperforin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the release of chemical signals that cause itching and inflammation, making it an effective ingredient for soothing itchy eyes. The flavonoids present in Hypericum perforatum creams have antioxidant properties that help protect the eye area from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing inflammation and itching. By using Hypericum perforatum creams, individuals can experience relief from itchy eyes due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Add the dried Hypericum perforatum flowers to the mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the flowers.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Hypericum perforatum cream is ready to use for itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis creams contains bioactive constituents like arbutin, ursolic acid, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, making it an effective treatment for itchy eyes. The ursolic acid in Euphrasia officinalis creams has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which help to calm irritated tissues and reduce itching. The flavonoids, on the other hand, have antioxidant properties that protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. By combining these properties, Euphrasia officinalis creams provides a safe and natural solution for itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Euphrasia officinalis herbal creams for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of Euphrasia officinalis tincture, and 2 tablespoons of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Combine the distilled water and Euphrasia officinalis tincture in a small bowl.
Add the carrier oil to the bowl and mix until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass container or jar.
Store the Euphrasia officinalis cream in the refrigerator and apply a small amount to itchy eyes as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains boiactive constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These compounds help to calm redness and reduce swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes. Linalool also has a sedative effect, which can help to reduce eye irritation and discomfort. The antiseptic properties of Melissa officinalis creams can also help to prevent infections that can cause itchy eyes, making it a useful treatment for this problem. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Melissa officinalis creams can provide quick relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Melissa officinalis leaves. You can find them in health food stores or online.
Combine the Melissa leaves with 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan. Melt them over low heat.
Combine the cooled Melissa infusion with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture. Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let it cool.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains a mixture of bioactive compounds, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The gel from the aloe vera plant also contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which can help to protect the eyes from damage and promote healing. Aloe-emodin, in particular, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and can help to reduce the production of inflammatory chemicals in the eyes. By applying aloe barbadensis cream to itchy eyes, these compounds can work together to provide relief from irritation and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds.
Add the Aloe barbadensis gel and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a bowl or container. Let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solid, scoop out the Aloe cream and store it in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus creams contains a compound called ingenol mebutate, which is also known as boiactive. Ingenol mebutate is a potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic that helps to reduce swelling and fight off infections in the eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of ingenol mebutate make it effective in soothing itchy eyes by reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, ingenol mebutate has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent eye infections that may be causing the itchiness. By reducing inflammation and preventing infections, Euphorbia peplus creams can provide relief for itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Euphorbia peplus herbal creams for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Euphorbia peplus leaves and flowers. Crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the Euphorbia peplus powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the coconut oil melts and the mixture is well combined.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Store the Euphorbia peplus cream in a cool, dark place. Use it as needed to soothe itchy eyes. Apply a small amount to the affected area and gently massage it in.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contain active constituents like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes. The gel-like substance in aloe vera, called mucilage, soothes and calms the eyes, providing relief from irritation. The antioxidants in aloe vera, including vitamins A and E, protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and soothing the eyes, aloe vera creams can provide quick relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, and 1 teaspoon of honey.
In a small bowl, mix the aloe vera gel and honey until well combined.
Add the vitamin E oil and coconut oil to the bowl and mix until smooth.
Transfer the mixture to a clean container and store it in the fridge for up to 1 week.
To use, dip a cotton swab into the mixture and gently apply it to itchy eyes. Avoid touching the eye area with the swab.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin and flavonoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes. Glycyrrhizin works by inhibiting the production of leukotrienes, which are chemicals that cause inflammation, while flavonoids have antioxidant properties that calm the skin and reduce irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids help to reduce the itching and discomfort associated with itchy eyes. By applying Glycyrrhiza glabra creams to itchy eyes, you can experience relief from itching and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for itchy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of glycerin, and 1/4 cup of powdered Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Boil the distilled water in a saucepan and let it cool down.
Mix 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of powdered Glycyrrhiza glabra root, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a small bowl.
Add the cooled-down distilled water to the bowl and mix well to create a smooth paste.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and harden, then use it as an eye cream for itchy eyes.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe itchy eyes. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can calm irritated eyes. Others, like nettle tea, are known to reduce swelling and itching. The antioxidants in herbal tea may also help protect your eyes from further irritation.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for itchy eyes daily may not be a good idea. Some herbs can cause allergic reactions or interact with other medications. Also, relying on herbal teas for constant relief might mask the underlying issue rather than addressing it. It's essential to use them in moderation and consider alternative treatments if symptoms persist.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with itchy eyes, but it's hard to say exactly when you'll feel relief. Some people notice a difference after a few days of drinking the tea, while others might take a week or two. It really depends on how often you drink the tea and how severe your itchy eyes are.
faq_time: For itchy eyes, try drinking herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the calming effects to last throughout the day. As the soothing ingredients start to work, your eyes might feel better, and you'll be more comfortable going about your daily activities.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for itchy eyes. Some herbal teas, like St. John's Wort, can affect the way your body processes certain medications, changing how they work. This can be especially true if you're taking other medications that have similar effects. Be cautious when drinking herbal teas with prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and have itchy eyes, you might be tempted to try herbal teas for relief. However, some herbal teas can be a concern during pregnancy. For example, chamomile and peppermint teas are generally considered safe, but others like sage and pennyroyal can be too strong for your body. It's best to check the ingredients and choose teas that are gentle on your system.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for itchy eyes are generally safe to consume while breastfeeding. However, it's essential to check the ingredients. Some teas, like chamomile and peppermint, are commonly used and considered safe. But teas with stronger herbs, like echinacea, may pass into breast milk and affect the baby. Always read the label carefully.
